rulings.Case Title: UNITED FARMERS AGENTS ASSOCIATION INC ON BEHALF VS. FARMERS GROUP INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 27, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT FARMERS GROUP INC, FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FARMERS NEW WORLD LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE, and, MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Motion for Judgment on the pleadings is granted. Ten days leave to amend to allege why this action is not time-barred. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL SUI MAN LAM et al VS. MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Transfer And Consolidate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 27, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF CAROL LAM, and, LAI LEE'S Motion To Transfer And Consolidate. Motion granted. The transfer will promote the ends of justice. Both cases arise from the same incident. The complaint in the San Francisco case and the cross-complaint in the Alameda case are substantially similar. These actions are not complex. Common question of law and facts is predominating and significant: the condition of the property before the incident, damages, and responsibility for payment of the damages. The parties, witnesses and counsel will not be inconvenienced by the transfer. Both cases are recently filed. Transfer would be in the interest of judicial economy, and eliminate the risk of inconsistent rulings, orders, or judgments. Coordination increses the likelihood of settlement of the actions. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: LELA MARTIN et al VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 27, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART, and, MARTIN LUTHER KING-MARCUS GARVEY SQUARE'S Motion For Attorney Fees. Motion for attorney's fees granted. Defendant is entitled to attorney fees pursuant to CCP section 425.16(c). Hourly rate reasonable, and time expended reasonable and necessary. Defendant awarded attorney fees in the Lodestar amount of $3,870. =(302/CWW/JW)
